subject: SCIENCE
title: Light Lets Us See
objective: Distinguish sources and reflectors and explain safe seeing.
introduction: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps. You will explore examples, sort cards, practice, and answer five questions.

vocabulary.definition: A key word used in the lesson “Light Lets Us See”.
A key word used in the lesson “Light Lets Us See”.
A key word used in the lesson “Light Lets Us See”.

activities.instruction: Study the idea and listen to the narration.
activities.content: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: Text alternative: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

activities.instruction: Explore each learning token, then read the examples.
activities.accessibilityAlternative: A numbered list of the same examples is available for keyboard and screen-reader use.

activities.instruction: Sort each card into “fits the lesson” or “does not fit.”

doesNotFit: Moon makes its own visible light

feedback.correct: Your groups follow the lesson rule.
feedback.retry: Use the explanation, not color or position.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Tap an item and then tap a labeled category; dragging is optional.

activities.instruction: Why can we see a book in a lit room?

options: Light reflects from it into our eyes.
Eyes send a beam into it.

feedback.correct: Correct: Light reflects from it into our eyes.
feedback.retry: Read the lesson rule again. The book makes sunlight. and Eyes send a beam into it. do not fit.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: All choices are visible text and selectable with TalkBack.

activities.instruction: Match each example to the lesson idea.

feedback.retry: Return to the explanation and compare one pair at a time.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: A list-based matching control replaces drag gestures.

activities.instruction: Complete the guided review in order.

feedback.correct: You are ready for the five-question check.

activities.accessibilityAlternative: Up and down buttons provide an alternative to dragging.

items.prompt: Which statement best matches “Light Lets Us See”?

options.text: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.
Moon makes its own visible light

items.explanation: The correct response is “We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.” because it follows the lesson explanation: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

options.text: Moon makes its own visible light

items.explanation: The correct response is “lamp—source” because it follows the lesson explanation: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

items.prompt: Which choice does not follow the lesson idea?

options.text: Moon makes its own visible light

items.explanation: The correct response is “Moon makes its own visible light” because it follows the lesson explanation: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

items.prompt: Why can we see a book in a lit room?

options.text: Light reflects from it into our eyes.
Eyes send a beam into it.

items.explanation: The correct response is “Light reflects from it into our eyes.” because it follows the lesson explanation: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

items.prompt: Which example should be used in the final review?

options.text: Moon makes its own visible light

items.explanation: The correct response is “book—seen by reflected light” because it follows the lesson explanation: We see an object when light from a source or reflected by the object enters our eyes. Never look at the Sun, lasers, or powerful lamps.

assetSpecs.prompt: Friendly Grade 3 cartoon storybook concept board for Light Lets Us See; rounded shapes, warm dark-brown outlines, tropical palette, no embedded text, uncluttered small-screen layout. Compose overlays provide labels and interactive targets.
assetSpecs.contentDescription: Five connected learning tokens for exploring examples in Light Lets Us See.

safetyNotes: Use the on-screen activity only; no unsupervised physical experiment is required.
